menu.name: Latte: +.50 Oat | Almond Milk, +.50 Syrup
menu.description: Locally roasted fresh coffee bean ground o make a double espresso blended with milk | syrup choice. Silky light foam tops it off.

menu.name: Cappuccino: +.50 Oat | Almond Milk, +.50 Syrup
menu.description: A Italian coffee drink made with espresso, steamed milk, and milk foam, creating a balanced, creamy, and velvety texture with a distinct coffee flavor. The drink's distinct layers and rich foam make it a popular and sophisticated choice.

menu.name: Flat White: +.50 Oat | Almond Milk, +.50 Syrup
menu.description: A flat white is a strong coffee drink from Australia/New Zealand, made with a double shot of espresso and velvety steamed milk with a thin layer of microfoam, resulting in a smooth, strong coffee flavor without the thick foam of a latte or cappuccino. It's served in a smaller cup (around 5-8 oz) and emphasizes a balanced, integrated texture where the espresso flavor is prominent but softened by the silky milk.

menu.name: Americano
menu.description: A Caffè Americano is a simple espresso-based drink made by adding hot water to one or two shots of espresso, resulting in a coffee with a similar strength to drip coffee but a different flavor profile. Its name comes from American soldiers in Italy during WWII who diluted strong espresso with water to make it more familiar to their tastes. It can be served hot or iced and is enjoyed black or with a splash of milk or cream.

menu.name: Cortado: +.50 Oat | Almond Milk, +.50 Syrup
menu.description: A cortado is a Spanish coffee drink made with equal parts espresso and steamed milk, creating a balanced, smooth beverage that "cuts" the espresso's acidity. It's typically served in a small glass (around 4-5 ounces) and has a 1:1 ratio of espresso to milk, making it stronger than a latte but smoother than a straight espresso shot. The name comes from the Spanish word cortar, meaning "to cut".

menu.name: Espresso
menu.description: Among the many forms of coffee, there’s a tiny but mighty option that has captured the hearts of caffeine enthusiasts—espresso. Often described as “a shot,” espresso is a concentrated coffee beverage that is brewed by forcing hot water through finely-ground coffee beans. This method results in a small, intense shot of coffee with a rich, concentrated flavor. Espresso can be enjoyed on its own or used in many other coffee beverages, such as cappuccinos, lattes, macchiatos, and more. Its versatility and creamy, flavorful taste make it a fan favorite among coffee lovers.

menu.name: Craft Lemonade (cold)
menu.description: Passion fruit, Hawaiian Tropic, Dragon Fruit, Cotton candy, Cupcake

menu.name: *Matcha
menu.description: Matcha is a finely ground powder of green tea specially processed from shade-grown tea leaves. Shade growing gives matcha its characteristic bright green color and strong umami flavor. Matcha is typically consumed suspended in hot water.
